summary:
The primary cause of leeway space stems from the larger sizes and distinct shapes of deciduous canines and molars compared to their permanent equivalents, facilitating proper eruption while minimizing initial crowding problems during dental development. \boxed{\text{Leeway space results mainly from the disparity between deciduous and permanent tooth dimensions}} Agent1's reasoning (Round 2):

- Emphasized that leeway space specifically relates to the significant variations in size and form between deciduous canines/molars versus their permanent versions.
- Highlighted this as a critical factor ensuring appropriate spacing before final positioning occurs post-late childhood/adolescence when all adult teeth have erupted fully.

Final Summary:

The main reason for leeway space lies in the substantial dimensional discrepancies between deciduous canines and molars relative to their mature substitutes; these disparities allow sufficient room until full occlusal alignment after most milk-teeth fall out. 

\boxed{
   Leeway space largely originates from the notable contrasts in size and morphology between deciduous canines/molars and their definitive counterparts,
ensuring adequate spatial accommodation throughout early dentition stages prior to complete emergence of permanent sets.
}

This summary captures both core aspects—specifically mentioning "canine" alongside its context with other types—and maintains clarity around developmental timing crucially linked to overall orthodontic considerations regarding eruptive dynamics.
